label: Minor splinter removed without retained foreign body or infection
inclusion_criteria: Adult with clinician diagnosis or discharge impression of minor superficial splinter removed.
No retained foreign body, deep structure injury, infection, bite/dirty wound, diabetic-foot risk, pregnancy/pediatric pathway, or specialist-directed plan.
exclusion_criteria: Retained foreign body, glass/metal or deep object concern, tendon/nerve/vessel/bone concern, joint violation, open fracture, dirty wound, bite wound, infection, neurovascular compromise, severe pain, or delayed high-risk presentation.
Diabetic-foot risk, immunocompromise, pregnancy/pediatric pathway, or specialist-directed wound/hand plan.
default_follow_up: Follow up with primary care, urgent care, wound care, or the ED if pain, redness, or foreign-body concern is not improving.

source_audit.notes: MedlinePlus supports cuts and puncture wound framing, deep-structure risk, infection warning signs, and retained-object concerns. Autonomous Phase 2101-2200 approved this narrow v1 only for clinician-diagnosed minor superficial splinter removal without retained foreign body, deep structure injury, infection, high-risk wound context, pregnancy/pediatric pathway, or specialist-directed plan.

review.notes: Reviewed as a narrow production ontology phenotype. MedlinePlus supports cuts and puncture wound framing, deep-structure risk, infection warning signs, and retained-object concerns. Autonomous Phase 2101-2200 approved this narrow v1 only for clinician-diagnosed minor superficial splinter removal without retained foreign body, deep structure injury, infection, high-risk wound context, pregnancy/pediatric pathway, or specialist-directed plan.
required_modifiers: adult with clinician diagnosis or discharge impression of minor superficial splinter removed
foreign body removed completely or no retained foreign body documented
no infection, deep structure injury, high-risk wound context, or specialist-directed plan documented
